IsA: It is a railroad board game.
It is part of the Empire Builder series.
HasA: It has players.
It has cities.
It has train lines.
It has cargo.
It has money.
It has European Currency Units (ECU).
It has Demand cards.
It has freight Loco cards.
It has train tracks.
It has mileposts.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by building and extending train lines.
It is characterized by delivering cargo.
It is characterized by earning money.
It is characterized by a game map.
It is characterized by wax crayons or other erasable markers.
It is characterized by terrain costs.
It is characterized by city costs.
It is characterized by river building costs.
It is characterized by ocean inlet or lake building costs.
It is characterized by ferry ports.
UsedFor: It is used for connecting cities.
It is used for delivering cargo.
It is used for earning money.
It is used for moving trains.
It is used for loading trains.
It is used for unloading trains.
It is used for paying fees.
It is used for collecting payment for deliveries.
It is used for building train tracks.
It is used for upgrading trains.
CreatedBy: It was created by Darwin Bromley.
It was created by Steven Courtemanche.
It was created by Larry Roznai.
PublishedIn: It was published by Mayfair Games.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Mayfair Games.
ConsistsOf: It consists of players.
It consists of cities.
It consists of train lines.
It consists of cargo.
It consists of money.
It consists of European Currency Units (ECU).
It consists of Demand cards.
It consists of freight Loco cards.
It consists of train tracks.
It consists of mileposts.
PartOf: It is part of the Empire Builder series.
It is part of the "Crayon Rails" games.
KnownFor: It is known for its improved and refined design compared to its predecessors.
It is known for its accurate geography.
It is known for its use of the ECU.
It is known for being engrossing.
It is known for taking the better part of an evening to play.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Mayfair Games.
It is associated with Darwin Bromley.
It is associated with Steven Courtemanche.
It is associated with Larry Roznai.
It is associated with the Empire Builder series.
It is associated with British Rails.
It is associated with Australian Rails.
It is associated with Russian Rails.
It is associated with Iron Dragon.
It is associated with Lunar Rails.
WonBy: It was won by the Origins Award for Best Modern-Day Boardgame of 1990.
About: It is about connecting cities.
It is about building train lines.
It is about delivering cargo.
It is about earning money.
It is about European Currency Units (ECU).
It is about train speeds.
It is about train load capacities.
It is about demand cards.
It is about freight loco cards.
It is about upgrading trains.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Empire Builder.
